title: "I Want To Be Found Faithful"  II Timothy 4:8

notes.introduction: Preaching from 2 Timothy 4:8, this sermon calls believers to pursue faithfulness to God's calling throughout their lives so they may receive the crown of righteousness at Christ's appearing.
outline: I. Reaching Precedes Grasping — Paul's commitment to knowing and serving Christ, seen in his three statements: 'I have fought a good fight,' 'I have finished my course,' and 'I have kept the faith' (2 Tim. 4:6-7)

II. Obeying Precedes Rewarding — The crown is not earned by superior human effort but by faithful obedience to the specific calling and path God has given each believer, as God the righteous judge watches and keeps track

III. Occupying Precedes Appearing — Until Christ returns, every believer must use the gift God has given them in His service and guard against the world's distractions, as illustrated by the warning example of Demas (2 Tim. 4:10)
notes.conclusion: The preacher closed with the story of Colonel John B. Gordon holding the center line at Antietam despite five wounds, using it to illustrate that men give everything for causes that will not last. He charged the congregation that servants of the Lord have the opportunity to focus all they have on following God's path for them, so that when they stand before Christ and receive the crown of righteousness, they can cast it at His feet. His final call was for every hearer to purpose in their heart to be found faithful to the very end, no matter the wounds, difficulties, or distractions.
notes.application: Appreciate, protect, and participate in the church God has given you. Do more church, not less, as you see the day approaching. Make the focus of your life pursuing Jesus Christ, not what the world offers. Use the specific gift God has given you and occupy — keep using it in His service until He comes or calls for you. Guard your focus carefully; if something is distracting you or your family from the Lord, be willing to put it down. Purpose in your heart to be found faithful, and ask the Holy Spirit to search your heart and show you where you have lost focus so you can bring it back to the Lord.
